families.rule_subset_note: All Claude rules apply at default severity.
All Claude rules apply at default severity.
XML structuring + few-shot rules only; everything else downgraded to warn.
Generic Claude label — flag at warn for explicit version.
All GPT-5.5 rules apply at default severity.
Unknown model classification raises an error to force explicit model: in frontmatter.

rules.summary: Prompt file model: must match its target agent's model: when both are present.
rules.rationale: Mismatched models cause runtime selection drift; the prompt's frontmatter model: is the source the user sees in chat.

rules.summary: handoff entries should not redundantly set model: when it matches the target agent's own model.
rules.rationale: Redundant overrides become stale and prevent global model rollouts.

rules.summary: Large Claude agents (>350 body lines) should include <context_awareness> block.
rules.rationale: Anthropic recommends explicit context budgeting for long system prompts; <context_awareness> activates context-management runtime-compression routing.

rules.summary: Claude research agents should include <investigate_before_answering> block.
rules.rationale: Anthropic's recommended pattern for grounding agents that read code or external sources before answering.

rules.summary: ONE-SHOT Claude agents (Requirements, Challenger subagent) MUST NOT contain <investigate_before_answering>.
rules.rationale: ONE-SHOT agents complete work in a single round; the investigate block adds latency and conflicts with their bounded contract. False-positive guard for legacy-004.

rules.summary: GPT-5.5 agents must contain outcome-first skeleton sections: # Goal, # Success criteria, # Constraints, # Output, # Stop rules.
rules.rationale: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 prompting guide explicitly publishes this skeleton; missing sections degrade behavior calibration.

rules.summary: GPT-family agents MUST NOT contain Claude-specific XML blocks (<investigate_before_answering>, <context_awareness>, <scope_fencing>, <empty_result_recovery>, <subagent_budget>).
rules.rationale: OpenAI prompting guide steers GPT-5.5 with markdown sections; Claude-only XML blocks are noise that GPT models do not natively interpret.

rules.summary: Absolute words (ALWAYS, NEVER, MUST, HARD RULE) density should not exceed 0.05 outside permitted prose contexts (security baseline / governance / approval gate / non-negotiable).
rules.rationale: OpenAI guide: 'Avoid unnecessary absolute rules. Use those words for true invariants ... For judgment calls, prefer decision rules instead.' Anthropic 4.6+ models also overtrigger on aggressive language.

rules.summary: Agents/prompts using a deprecated model label (per validate-deprecated-models.mjs) should be migrated.
rules.rationale: Deprecated models lose vendor support and security updates; migration windows are often time-bound.

rules.summary: Claude agents/prompts MUST NOT instruct prefilling the assistant turn.
rules.rationale: Anthropic doc explicitly states: 'Prefilled responses on the last assistant turn are no longer supported. Starting with Claude 4.6 models and Mythos Preview, requests with prefilled assistant messages return a 400 error.' Prefill instructions silently break Claude 4.6+ deployments.

rules.summary: GPT-5.5 agents must have a non-empty body under # Stop rules (>=1 non-blank line before next H1).
rules.rationale: OpenAI prompting guide: 'Add explicit stopping conditions ... Define missing-evidence behavior'. An empty Stop rules section is misleading scaffolding.

rules.summary: .agent.md must use array form for model:; .prompt.md must use string form.
rules.rationale: YAML bareword form misparses parenthetical qualifiers (e.g., a hypothetical 'Claude Opus 4.7 (some suffix)' label) and breaks frontmatter loading. Array vs string is the canonical convention for each file type.

rules.summary: Claude agents that produce a formal artifact MUST include an <output_contract> block.
rules.rationale: Anthropic recommends XML-tagged structured output for agents with defined deliverables. Heuristic: handoff prompts referencing agent-output/ paths or known artifact-producing names.

rules.summary: Every handoffs[].prompt must contain BOTH an input reference (artifact path or 'Input:') AND an output reference (save path or 'Output:').
rules.rationale: Multi-agent workflows lose state when handoffs omit input/output context; receiving agents waste tokens reconstructing what they need.

rules.summary: # Personality block should appear only on user-facing agents (Orchestrator family); internal pipeline agents should omit it.
rules.rationale: OpenAI guide: 'For customer-facing assistants, support workflows, coaching experiences, and other conversational products, define both personality and collaboration style.' Pipeline subagents have no user-facing surface; personality is noise.

rules.summary: Prompts targeting a custom agent MUST NOT declare model:; prompts using agent: agent (or no agent:) MUST declare an explicit model:.
rules.rationale: When a prompt targets a custom agent (e.g., `agent: "02-Requirements"`), the agent's own model: is the source of truth — duplicating it on the prompt creates drift risk. When a prompt is generic (`agent: agent`), there is no inherited model, so an explicit model: is mandatory for the validator to classify the family. The validator resolves effective family via the target agent when the prompt omits model:.
